intent: Replace dialog-based edit and delete operations on learning-path pages with inline editing (click-to-edit) and immediate-delete-plus-undo-toast pattern. Adds reusable InlineEditableField component, deletePathWithUndo/restorePath store methods with 5-second undo window, and updates both LearningPaths (list) and LearningPathDetail pages.
reviewers: reliability -- new store methods with setTimeout/clearTimeout lifecycle, async persist with syncableWrite, error handling for Dexie failures
adversarial -- 956 insertions of executable TypeScript/React code, data mutation operations (delete with undo/restore)
kieran-typescript -- TypeScript components, store, hooks, React patterns
julik-frontend-races -- DOM event handling (click propagation, blur timing, setTimeout for auto-focus), async UI state transitions

appliedFixes.fix: Added focusTimerRef and successTimerRef to store timer IDs; added useEffect cleanup return to clear timers on unmount

findings.title: restorePath persist is fire-and-forget; state and Dexie can diverge on write failure
findings.why_it_matters: restorePath calls persistWithRetry() without await, then clears pendingDeletes immediately. If the Dexie write fails, the in-memory state shows the path restored but the persistence layer does not have it. On next loadPaths() the path silently disappears. The user sees a restored path with a 'Failed to restore path' toast, which is contradictory and confusing.
findings.suggested_fix: Await persistWithRetry() and only clear pendingDeletes on success. In the catch block, keep pendingDeletes entry so the user can retry Undo (the path remains visible in state). Only show 'Failed to restore path' when persist actually fails.
evidence: Line 366-368: delete pendingDeletes[pathId]; set({ pendingDeletes }) -- executes synchronously before persist resolves
Line 360-362: .catch(error => { console.error(...); toast.error('Failed to restore path') }) -- error handled but state already cleared

findings.title: Textarea mode has no newline support; Enter always saves
findings.why_it_matters: The handleKeyDown handler treats Enter as save for both input and textarea modes. For the description textarea (used on path cards and detail page), users may reasonably expect Enter to insert a line break and Ctrl+Enter/Cmd+Enter to save. The current behavior prevents any multi-line descriptions from being created via the inline editor.
findings.suggested_fix: For textarea mode, allow Enter to insert newlines and use Ctrl+Enter or Cmd+Enter to save. Alternatively, document in the aria-label that Enter saves.
evidence: Line 77: if (e.key === 'Enter' && !e.shiftKey) { e.preventDefault(); commitSave() } -- applies to both input and textarea
Plan R2: 'Path description on path cards becomes click-to-edit' -- no mention of multi-line editing behavior
The description field on LearningPathDetail uses as='textarea' with maxLength=500 -- long text benefits from paragraph structure

findings.title: _finalizeDelete is public API despite private naming convention
findings.why_it_matters: The _finalizeDelete method is declared in the exported LearningPathState interface (line 180: _finalizeDelete: (pathId: string) => Promise<void>). Any component or test can call it directly. The _ prefix signals intent but provides no enforcement. Tests already call it directly (useLearningPathStore.getState()._finalizeDelete(pathId)).
evidence: Line 180: _finalizeDelete: (pathId: string) => Promise<void> -- declared in public interface

findings.title: Unsafe ref cast narrows union type for input/textarea
findings.why_it_matters: inputRef is typed as useRef<HTMLInputElement | HTMLTextAreaElement>(null) but cast to narrower types (React.Ref<HTMLTextAreaElement> and React.Ref<HTMLInputElement>) via 'as'. TypeScript cannot verify the cast is correct at compile time. If the component logic ever mismatches as='textarea' with the input branch, the ref type would be wrong and could cause runtime errors.
findings.suggested_fix: Use separate refs for input and textarea, or a callback ref pattern. The current approach works correctly but loses type safety.
evidence: Line 148: ref={inputRef as React.Ref<HTMLTextAreaElement>} -- unsafe cast
Line 175: ref={inputRef as React.Ref<HTMLInputElement>} -- unsafe cast

residualRisks: Page refresh during undo window: path is removed from state but not finalized in Dexie. On reload, the path reappears. This is intentional per the plan.
Dexie on('changes') propagation across tabs: if a path is deleted in Tab A, Tab B may still show it until a store reload. This is a pre-existing concern, not introduced by this PR.
restorePath persist failure (F1): if Dexie write fails, path appears restored but will vanish on reload. See finding F1.
learnings: None found -- docs/solutions/ search returned no relevant past solutions for inline editing or undo-delete patterns
agentNativeGaps: No agent-native gaps found. The InlineEditableField component and deletePathWithUndo are internal UI/store changes that don't introduce new user-facing actions requiring agent tool equivalents.
testingGaps: No test for Space key activation on read-only text (the keyDown handler supports both Enter and Space but only Enter is tested)
No test for rapid edit-save-edit sequence (stale state risk mentioned in Unit 2 test scenarios)

coverage.untrackedFilesExcluded: Several .context/, docs/plan/, and docs/brainstorm/ files are untracked but are CE pipeline artifacts.
